title: Bridge OpenAI synthesis evaluator suggestions to instruction-packet draft tickets
problem: After the live OpenAI synthesis evaluator produces a public-safe verdict and remediation suggestions, there is no explicit bridge from that evaluator artifact into the existing autonomous synthesis governor instruction-packet and draft-ticket handoff. Without the bridge, a plan or implementation agent must manually translate evaluator findings into builder-ready scope.
acceptance_criteria: Evaluator GO/PARTIAL/NO-GO artifacts can be converted into an instruction packet that names the problem, evidence artifact, affected modules, expected files, acceptance criteria, test plan, non-goals, risk level, and rollback plan.
Instruction-packet ticket drafts remain gitignored under data/operator/draft_tickets/ and are never auto-promoted to tickets/ or TICKET_QUEUE.md.
The bridge reuses existing instruction_packet_ticket_draft validation rules rather than creating a parallel ticket schema.
Generated draft tickets include forbidden_actions such as auto_merge, auto_push, auto_promote_ticket, and edit_TICKET_QUEUE.
Unit tests prove evaluator NO-GO creates a scoped draft improvement ticket and evaluator GO can either produce no draft or produce a documented follow-up without unsafe automation.
non_goals: Do not let OpenAI, evaluator output, or instruction packets directly edit source code, run shell commands, use Git, or write accepted graph rows.
Do not auto-promote generated drafts into canonical queue tickets.
Do not add public write routes, public source ingestion routes, or public agent execution routes.
Do not require live OpenAI for unit tests.
rollback_plan: Remove the evaluator-to-instruction bridge and related tests while preserving the standalone evaluator artifact from ticket-394 and the existing governor/draft-ticket flow.
